response: Execution-ready handoff prepared for OpenCode Workflow Policy Layer. Source-of-truth artifacts: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-09-opencode-workflow-policy-layer-design.md, docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-09-opencode-workflow-policy-layer-implementation-plan.md, docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-09-opencode-workflow-policy-layer-tasks.md, docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-09-opencode-workflow-policy-layer-implement.md. Execution shape: multi-lane coding. Goal: add minimal workflow state + step intents + intent-to-lane resolution + callback-driven isolated continuation + observability + tests. Keep implementation minimal/additive and fail-fast. Do not build a heavyweight workflow DSL in this pass.

prompt: Workflow Policy Layer review of in-progress implementation
response: Outer-agent review of in-progress Workflow Policy Layer implementation: keep the new modules src/workflow-policy.ts, src/step-intent-resolver.ts, and src/workflow-state.ts. They are aligned with the approved architecture and should be preserved. However, registrar.ts is taking on too much workflow continuation logic and is beginning to turn into a god-file. Before commit, refactor continuation/workflow transition logic out of registrar.ts into helper modules. Recommended split: (A) src/workflow-continuation.ts for resolveContinuationStep, buildContinuationTransitionDedupeKey, launchWorkflowContinuationStep; (B) src/workflow-callback-transition.ts for handling launch_run/notify/stop/escalate actions and patching continuation state. registrar.ts should remain the ingress/coordinator, not the full workflow engine. Legacy continuation coexistence is acceptable only as a fallback for runs without workflow metadata; workflow policy must remain primary when workflow state exists.
